He expects a lot of you, you need to work hard, but if you are interested in computer science, he will get you to where you need to be.  Great professor.  He cares a lot, and his grading is fair, despite how hard his tests are.
Okay guy. Grading is weird.

Great professor. I didnt have any programming experience but passed with an A. Provides clear examples in class and plenty of practice programs online. Dont be stupid tho, he challenges you with his tests so dont assume its enough to just go to class. pay attention in class, take time on the hw, and review concepts by messing with some programs.

Arup is the best professor I've been taught by so far. He makes his tests challenging to ensure that students study, but he has an extremely fair curving system. If you're struggling, he will go out of his way to break down something step by step. Definitely looking to take more classes with him. TAKE HIM!

This class was SOOOO hard for me.  I tried very hard, and thankfully I passed.  Others had no problem, but I am sure they had more programming experience than I did.  The tests are majorly curved.  He is super nice and helpful. Make sure you visit his office reg.  if you are in one of his classes.

Arup is without a doubt the most interested Professor I've had. He genuinely cares about his students and his interest in the material is infectious. It's not an easy class, but that's not his doing - it's tough material. Curves generously, but not so much so that you can't try. No textbook use. Take Arup, it's really that simple.

Arup is one of the best teachers at UCF. His homework and tests are difficult because he forces you to think in depth about the material he goes over.  I got a B in the class and I wouldn't consider myself an efficient programmer.  This class does require hard work, but you will learn.

Arup is one of the best professors I have come across in my three years of college! He is clear and goes at a great pace. You MUST go to class though. It's not that bad, I'm sure you can endure it. Go to TAs for help on assignments.  Grading is very fair!! He sets his own grade lines depending on the class's performance. Go to cs.ucf.edu/~dmarino. You'll find past grades, comments from students and everything you want to know about arup! I definitely recommend this professor! Take him!
He is a great lecturer and gives hard homework, but you are guaranteed to learn in his class. He is extremely clear in explanations.

If you have experience with C (or other programming) this class will probably be easy. If you haven't had any it can be very tough at times. His tests are tricky, get use to the way he likes to ask output and tracing questions before your first test. Programs are easy in the beginning than get difficult, and take alot of time to write.

Horrible Teacher. He is easy if you know programming before hand, but if you don't you will struggle. Tests are very tricky. He loves to put tricks in his questions. Programs are alright but get hard near the end. Overall, I recommend not taking him if you are new to C

He is very helpful. Maybe a little too helpful. He will sometimes come late to class because he was helping students in his class. He does not show up to every class mainly because he is sooooooo busy. I never used his book although his test used open books. I still ended up with an A. Just attend class and he spoon feeds you the material.

Lecturs are very personal, this guy isn't a concrete face, actually has personality. Don't need the book or really need to attend lectures, class was pretty easy. If you've already taken upper division math courses and have an engineering/mathematics mindset, you'll do fine. If you're a retarded business or IT major then you'll suck.

I've had Guha for 4 different classes now and his methods go across all of them. He's not easy, by any means, but he is the first professor at UCF I've felt TRULY cared about his students. He's brilliant, very personable, and is just an all around good guy. I don't care what grade you get, you'll walk out of his class having learned something.
This guy is the man!

This class was hard for me. But keep in mind I had no previous programming experience and very little interest(took it b/c I had to) compared to other prof he's prob the best teacher, but he's test are Hard. 2test plus final and 6 programming assignments. Dont buy the book, everything for his class is on line.

Awesome Professor!! He is extermely clear and he simplifies EVERYTHING so its very understandable. Is funny at times, but always cool, and never loses focus off teaching. Definetly take him over any other professor for ANY computer science class. Tests are kida hard and some easy, but thats expected.
Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered.

Cool guy, seems like he has a lot of fun in the class.  Uses a curved grading scale based on the class average.  Programming assignments can be challenging, but you should expect that anyway.  Definitely take Arup for every class possible!

So, this is my second class with Arup, and he is a pretty good instructor. Dumbs the lectures down enough for people like me without a strong CS background- explains everything in (sometimes excruciating) detail. Love his style of teaching,and would definitely take his class again.
Good guy, drank with at a tailgating event (gave me free beer), test are hard, and tricky. Dont try to share work, really smart guy he will catch you.
Nice guy. Fun class. gives money to volunteers. exams aren't too bad but need to reason over questions a couple times, he sometimes likes to trick you. overall great professor

Awesome guy. Very funny and provides detailed, thorough examples of concepts. I have no prior experience with programming and, though I find the class very challenging, it is nothing you can't pass with at least a C, unless you aren't trying.
One of the best professors I've ever had.  Courses are challenging, but fair.  Really speaks clearly and at a pace that is easy to follow.
best teacher in cs dept. as easy as cs gets, which is not that easy.

Arup Guha is the bomb.  He makes programming so easy and you will learn alot.  If you already have programming background in C then don't take the class because he goes at a kind of slow pace which is good for beginners.  I def would recommend him and for any other courses!

Pretty nerdy, yet cool guy. He's very knowledable. If you go to class, he does a great job at explaining the material. His tests are fair. He "curves" your grade at the end of the semester. I think a 74ish will get u a B. This is a tough course, but he makes it really clear. I recommend him over any other programming proffesor.
Good lecture notes, but all the math on the tests got really annoying.
He get's really giddy and excited about most anything.

This is a hard class and he can be a hard teacher, but he is much better than almost all CS teachers. He cares about his students and will help you whenever you need it. Curves the tests which is great. This class is pretty hard compared to CS1 so you need to put effort into it, but he makes it easier. Got a B which isn't bad considering I'm lazy.

very like-able person, but was disappointed with him this semester. grading was bizarre with no correlation to effort and assignment value had no relation to amount of effort to complete. i enjoyed the material but did not appreciate the vagueness & lack of communication in assignments which often lead to poor grades  due to his pass/fail mentality

This class was a lot of work, but you learn the material.  I finished with an A, but I still feel his grading scale is lazy and unfair.  Everything is graded relatively which does not allow everyone to pass with As.  Over half of the class received Cs.  The assignments take a lot of time, but are really helpful.  Do not bother buying the book.

Arup for the win! Unlike Intro. to C Programming, you will probably want to have the book for Intro. to OO, because the exams contain material which forces you to reference the API, unless you are extremely familiar with it already. The exams are fairly difficult, but as usual, Arup adjusts the grading scale to match the exams' difficulty.
Possibly the best CS professor in UCF. Writes helpful notes, great speaker, and does grades based on class average and SDs.

The best math teacher I've ever had. Thought his class was impossible on the first day. Turned out to be much better. Hard class, make sure to review notes online, Tests are hard. Thank God the class is curved. Careful on quizzes. GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS. If you don't go to class, you WILL fail.

Very clear lectures and sounded genuinely interested in the content.  Homework was done in groups of 2 and fairly difficult.  Final exam had some curveball questions, but other two exams were pretty good.  Overall, an enjoyable class and grade was based on mean/median of the class.

Great teacher, hard tests, but the grading scale is adjusted to make up for it.  Uses examples you can actually understand and relate to.  Definitely take him over the other two, you'll actually learn!
He is a great teacher.  Both him and his father are two of the best CS teachers at our school.   Take their classes if you have a chance.   Very fair teachers.
Arup is a great teacher! This is a class where you will actually learn! Very Hard Tests But Huge Curve at the end!! This guy is great!

This is my second time with Arup, and once again he did fairly well. Most of the time he was spot on, but ocassionally there were flubs or he would outright say he doesn't know this part of java very well. Regardless he tries his best, and will not test you on something he didn't teach well. Grades are VERY curved, lenient guy. He was usually late.

EXCELLENT teacher! one of my favorite at ucf. the notes are posted online but going to class makes it so much easier to understand. exams are fairly hard and the programs are challenging but fun. i suggest him 100% if possible.

Arup was always my favorite CS teacher but I dont recommend him for CS3. Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered. 40% of the first test was based on solving 2 rather tricky programming problems that werent discussed in class at all. Hes still a good teacher and I recommend him for anything below CS3.

This guy is such a great professor.  The first bunch of programming assignments are easy, the last few are kind of hard, but he grades really easy (something like a 45% in the class is a C) The MC parts of his tests are really tricky, but he gives out a free answer with the last question everytime Free reponse is a piece of cake. Will take him agai

Great guy, great teacher.  Highly recommended.  I don't think I would have ever liked the subject so much but for his teaching.  Many students find his exams and assignments hard, but he will help you understand the material.  C programming can be challenging and confusing to some of us, and this is the best teacher to learn from.

Very nice guy. Takes a lot to get an A in the course though as his exams are very challenging, but he's a nice guy. Do his work, show up to class (he takes attendance) and do the homework and you should be fine.

Arup is a fantastic teacher! His tests and homework assignments are challenging, but you learn so much from them. He also has tremendous respect and concern for his students. He isn't out to fail students, so it is easy to pass if you pay attention.

Really good teacher, I cannot stress enough take this professor. He is a really nice guy and acutally teaches the material in a method so you can understand it, also goes over tests and programming assignments and what was suppose to be done on them. Seriously take him you will have a great time.
DO NOT take him for topics!!!! Usually there are two sections of this course.  Guha's course requires WAY more work then either Orooji or Leeson. Try a different section.

Arup has been both my favorite and my most difficult professor. You never really grasped the concepts until doing the assignments, but there were not a lot of assignments at all (however, I wouldn't ask for more!). Arup was VERY helpful during office hours, and I would definitely recommend him (he speaks english.)

hes a cool teacher and likes to do a lot of beer examples lol. the material is covered completely so if u pay attention you should be ok. the only problem i had is the learning environment was horrible plus the lack of assignments made grasping the material difficult. but, hes a good teacher so id recommend him over others.

Insanely hard, but worth it.  I usually like to skate by with minimal effort whether I learn anything or not.  With Arup its a different story.  He challenges you and is very fair about it.  Always willing to help.  Makes CS interesting.

Very good teacher.  Knows what he's talking about, and can handle almost any question thrown at him.  Those with previous programming experience might be a tad bored, but he covers all the material very thoroughly.  Grades are kind of sparse, but he curves (grading philosophy is on his website).  Definitely a good professor to choose if available.

Great professor. It's Comp Sci, so don't expect an easy class, but he makes learning worth it. Often explains topics so they are easy to understand. A lot of programming homework, but in pairs (two people).  As always, do the work and you'll be fine.
Probably the coolest professor I've ever met. He's a good teacher, a really smart guy, and real laid back. He projects well to the class as well.

I wish I had Arup for CS1 and CS2, because I learned the most from his lectures and assignments in CS3.  Tests are exceptionally hard, but he grades extremely fair (a 60 may equate to a B).  All around great instructor.

Arup Guha is one of the best instructors at UCF.  His class is challenging, but he more concerned with your learning of the information than the grades.  He is a fair teacher, who is easy to approach and is willing to help as long as your willing to help yourself as well.  He is one of the only instructors that has not pushed me to a TA for aid.
IT majors will have a very hard time with him. Unless of course you are getting a minor or already have a BS degree in mathematics.
good notes, basically gives you the code for programming assignments beforehand. tests are hard, but liberal curve is always applied, usually A>=80.
Disrespectful and immature.  His teaching does nothing to prepare you for the tests he gives.  Stay away from this guy.
really helpful.If you don't miss any class and go over the notes at home, you will get an A for sure.
Don't take his classes
Biased and judgemental. Avoid if you can.

Guha is the best programming teacher at UCF. Some of the veteran professors could learn a thing or two from him. Guha's test are hard, but he genuinely cares for all of his students. He is always available in his office, and he will help you out grade wise as long as he sees that you are trying. Take him for any CS classes that you can.

Somtimes the notes were not completely clear, and more was added to them during the class so make sure to take notes.  The tests were hard, and although a study sheet is allowed, it doesn't really help.  Class is curved at the end.
horrible professor...if you dont get it his way then you are SOL.  I think he is the most unfair professor that I have ever had at UCF.

Very very helpful, very clear.  Gives tons of examples that he mostly comes up with himself.  I had no previous programming knowledge and got an A-.  Grades according to class performance, not material.
This was my second class with him, a very easy professor to understand, but kind of hard class.

Very good professor.  You will be bored stiff if you already know the material, but he really knows how to break it down for everyone else.  He will always stop what he is doing during office hours for you and he and his TA's always answer emails.

The material isn't easy, but he does a good job in going over it. I had no computer programming experience beforehand and got a B. The beginning of the class is very easy and the last two or three weeks are hard. Be sure to do well on the tests so you dont have to worry about the last 2 assignments.

Dont listen to these uneducated fools who say he is hard.  He is only hard if you cannot program at all and have no place being in CS to begin with.  He is one of the best CS teachers at UCF and I would highly recommend him to anyone.  Tests are hard but fair, as well as the programming assignments.

This was my second class with Arup, and he didn't dissappoint what I had come to know and love about his teaching style.  He's expert at explaining tough concepts, and you've gotta love a teacher that WANTS you to call them by their first name.

He makes great lecture notes that he posts online, stresses the important concepts in class, and gives ample examples that are often amusing. Tests are tricky, but as long as you do the sample ones he provides, you know what is coming. He is very helpful if you ask. Great instructor!

Even though my class was only once a week for three hours he managed to provide an ample amount of samples for his assignments. His grading policy is one that I'd recommend to all the other teachers.He did put a few problems on one of his tests before he taught the material but overall a great teach
A very bad professor.  His tests were a nightmare and well beyond what was taught in class.

I also had him for Intro to C and i work for him.  Hes a really nice guy and really helpfull.  And you can understand him, and as most CS majors know that its hard to find a professor that you can understand.

This is my first programming class, so having no previous knowlege, I was a bit worried.  Guha made everything very easy to understand, and made even the most complex topics seem easy.  Tests may seem tricky, but just look over sample tests he gives you before the real thing. Grading policy nice too
I felt he was a horrible teacher and that his tests were unfair.  I would never take him again and think everyone should avoid him.

Amazing teacher, he has a way of explaining complex topics in a manner that allows you to understand it the 1st time you hear it and most importantly allows you to remember it. I didn't buy the book, never studied, and got an A both class I had him for.

He is very easy to understand and talk to.  Assignments are usually short.  Need to come to class as much as possible, even if you know C!!!!  Gives make-up exams/quizzes without much question.  If you found the class hard, you prolly skipped alot.

He gives extremely difficult programs and tests.  You can expect all kinds of curves and tricks with him.  If you dont understand it exactly as he intends it, you get nowhere.  I know several people who have changed majors because of him.

Very good teacher, covers the material very well and speaks clearly. He knows what he is talking about.  His tests are very difficult, a lot of unexpected twists on the test.  Programs are a little bit hard but he gives you enough time to do them.
His lectures are very clear and structured.  He's a really good teacher but his tests are incredibly tough!  He uses his own grading scale (determined at the end of the semester).
Speaks clearly, uses a lot of examples, programs are a tad over-board. Don't this course along with another programming course.
Awesome CS1 Teacher, very smart, and actually explains the material very well. Class is tough, but if you study you should be good.

Most helpful/explanatory programming teacher I've ever had. Grades on tests/programs may be bad, but he curves accordingly and generously. Really helps students understand material. I will take more courses with him if at all possible. I recommend!
Extremely nice and helpful, he may be the best in the CS department...at the same time, his tests are very difficult and are based almost completley on reading trick questions. Huge curve tho.

You may think you have an understanding of the material, but he throws massive curves on the tests which leave you asking where he got this.  If you are willing, learn with another professor and dont waste your time.

Excellent teacher, but can be hard on tests.  Arup teaches a lot more about C and tries to make sure that the whole class understands what he teaches that day.  However, if you're not willing to learn, you shouldn't bother.
if you dont get it his way, you wont get any credit on the tests.  Think like him or else!
unreasonably hard
No Comments

Arup is not only one of the best CS teachers I have had, he is also one of the coolest. He pushes students to work harder but also explains concepts fully using concepts that we already understand. Great guy, I take as many classes from him as I can! :)

class is the hard no doubt but he is good at what he does but if its ur first programming class you may want to save your GPA now but if you do take it study study study and take advantage of all of the TA's office hours they were the only reason i passed
Class is curved a lot.  If you practice, you will do well.  If you know C, you don't have to go to class :)  Just keep up with the homework, and go to the reviews, and you'll get an A

He was great at explaining the C language. If you attend class you WILL understand how to program in C, he goes over in depth EVERY aspect of the code and techniques he expects you to know. Tests are definetly tricky but he gives you a massive curve.

He is very good at keeping such a boring class fun. He would make a good babysitter too, so many whiney kids in the class needing their noses wiped. By the way if you are a know it all about programming type, keep your mouth shut and just nod already!
Good intro to comp sci class

Arup is a hard tester. He is an excellent teacher. He does a superb job of explaining things and showing how things work. He makes students into real CS majors who actually know stuff instead of just get by. he spends money on food for us too! woohoo
Best teacher in the department, bar none.  Too bad you can't take all of your CS from him...you'd actually learn something
great teacher ... should take more grad classes .. wish i had some one like him in my undergrad classes..makes the tough class so fun and easy to understand....
ST: Cryptography.  Hard class but great teacher.  I never fell asleep in his class... that's very good!

One of the top 3 CS teachers I had during my undergrad at UCF. It's hard to appriciate how good of a teacher Arup is; until you've experienced a poor teacher teaching the same subject. Really cares about teaching, and it shows. He speaks perfect english.
This is such a hard class! I had never programmed before and felt really stupid the first few weeks. He is an extrememly nice teacher and helps you out a lot though. You definately have to work hard.
tests make your brain hurt but are curved heavily.
His class is very difficult, but he tries to make it easier to understand.  His class notes are posted on the web and grades on a curve.  Stick with the class, but you can't be lazy.

EXTREMELY HARD!! If you haven't taken a programming class before than you are in trouble. I had to WITHDRAWAL from it after the first program and test because my grades were sooo low... If you turn anything in wrong like .doc instead of .c you get a -1!!

Had him for COP3503, COP3530, COT3100, and now COT4810.  Extremely approachable, friendly, and honestly cares about whether or not his students learn.  Brings confusing concepts down to a level that is understandable (ex. 12 permutations of the word beer)
Awesome teacher.  Puts class notes online.  Very difficult class, but he is extremely good at presenting the material.  Perfect english.
Had for cs2, cs3, and discrete.  Very good teacher.  One of the best by far.  Cool Cool guy.  Hard classes and hard tests, but he grades on class average.
No Comments
